actions.name: Set acceleration
actions.description: Set the corresponding properties. See Car properties for more information.

actions.name: Set deceleration
actions.description: Enable or disable the movement. If disabled, the movement no longer has any effect on the object.

actions.name: Set default controls
actions.description: Set whether input is being ignored. If input is ignored, pressing any of the control keys has no effect. However, unlike disabling the behavior, the object can continue to move.

actions.name: Set drift recover
actions.description: Set the current speed the object is moving at, in pixels per second.

actions.name: Set friction
actions.description: Simulate one of the movement controls being held down. Useful when disabling Default controls. See the behavior reference summary for more information.

actions.name: Set max speed
actions.description: A shortcut for setting the speed to zero.

conditions.name: Compare speed
conditions.description: Compare the current speed of the car, in pixels per second.

conditions.name: Is enabled
conditions.description: Test if the behavior is currently enabled. When disabled it will have no effect on the object.

conditions.name: Is moving
conditions.description: True if the current speed is non-zero. Invert to test if the car is stopped.

expressions.name: Acceleration
expressions.description: Return the corresponding properties. See Car properties for more information.

expressions.name: Deceleration
expressions.description: Get the current angle of motion (which can be different to the object's angle), in degrees.

expressions.name: DriftRecover
expressions.description: Get the current object's movement speed, in pixels per second.

expressions.name: Friction
expressions.description: Get the object's current speed on each axis, in pixels per second. For example, if the object is moving to the left at 100 pixels per second, VectorX is -100 and VectorY is 0.
